themes.title: Forgeline — Industrial & Trade Supplier HTML Template
themes.oneliner: A premium website template that actually understands the trade — datasheets, photometry downloads, quote requests and next-day delivery messaging, not another generic "business theme".
themes.description:
Most templates make an electrical wholesaler look like a startup. Forgeline was
designed *from inside the industry*: the demo content is a believable UK
commercial lighting distributor, with product spec tables (IP ratings, lumen
output, efficacy), a downloads tab for datasheets and LDT/IES photometric
files, trade-account CTAs, volume-break pricing and a "send us your bill of
materials" quote flow.

- 8 pages: Home, About, Products (filterable grid), Single Product, Services, Blog, Article, Contact
- HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS — open index.html and it just works, no build step
- Rebrand in minutes: every colour and font is a CSS variable
- Real photography included; product images as clean technical renders
- Vanilla JS only: tabs, filters, gallery, accordion, working demo forms
- WCAG-AA accessibility patterns, tested at iPhone widths, no horizontal scroll anywhere
- Plain-English README covering setup, rebranding and going live

Perfect for: electrical wholesalers, lighting suppliers, manufacturers,
engineering firms, industrial distributors, builders' merchants.

themes.title: Callout — Electrician Website Template (local trades)
themes.oneliner: A complete website for a local electrician — built around what actually wins trade work: reviews, fixed-quote promises, photos of real jobs, and a phone number everywhere.
themes.description:
Five pages (Home, Services, Recent Work, About, Free Quote) with a fully
written demo company so you see a real trade business, not lorem ipsum: trust
strip with guarantees, 6 service cards with honest trade copy, project cards
with budgets, review cards ready for your Checkatrade quotes, an areas-covered
strip, and a quote form with job-type dropdown.

- HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS — open index.html and it just works, no build step
- Rebrand in minutes: every colour and font is a CSS variable
- Vanilla JS only, WCAG-AA accessibility patterns, tested at iPhone widths
- Plain-English README covering setup, rebranding and going live

themes.title: Sideline — Grassroots Junior Football Club Template
themes.oneliner: The first club website template built for how junior clubs actually work — 20+ teams, boys' and girls' pathways, volunteers and safeguarding, no "first team" your club doesn't have.
themes.description:
Junior clubs don't need a fixtures-and-league-table site — they need parents to
find the right team, register in five minutes, and trust the setup. Sideline
is organised around exactly that:

- A **teams directory** with 24 demo teams across U6–U16, filterable by Boys / Girls / Mixed, each card showing league, format (4v4 → 11v11), training night, coach and live space availability
- Registration page with a fees table, sibling discounts and hardship fund — plus a parents' FAQ that answers the questions you get every July
- A volunteers page, because the real shortage is coaches, not players
- Safeguarding built in structurally: welfare contact first on the contact page, no junior players named, photo-consent language modelled
- 8 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Terrace — Football Club HTML Template (claret & sky)
themes.oneliner: A complete football club website in claret & sky (plus a 5-way colour switcher) — fixtures, results, league table, squad profiles, match reports and academy trials, styled for your colours out of the box.
themes.description:
Your club's website shouldn't look like a corporate brochure with a ball
photo. Terrace is a complete club site in **claret & sky (plus a 5-way colour switcher)** — the demo club
(Ravenshaw Rovers, est. 1921) wears your end of the colour spectrum so you can picture
your own badge on it immediately.

- **Match centre**: fixtures, results with scorelines and W/D/L badges, and a league table with your club's row highlighted — all plain HTML, edited by copy-paste
- **Live kick-off countdown** on the homepage (set one date attribute)
- **Squad grid** with position filter and player profile page (with a "sponsor this player" revenue CTA)
- Match report layout, club history/honours, academy trials form with parents' FAQ, contact with matchday directions
- A proper **club crest** included as a recolourable SVG — swap in your own badge whenever you're ready
- 9 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA patterns, tested at iPhone widths
- Every colour is a CSS variable — matching your exact club colours is a five-minute job, documented in the README

Perfect for: non-league and semi-pro clubs, community clubs, Sunday league
sides, club volunteers who got handed "the website job".

Can't see your colours? Terrace is the any-colour edition: five ready-made colourways ship in the box (claret, royal, scarlet, forest, black & amber) switchable with one line of HTML — plus a live preview page — and the README shows how to hit your exact colours with one CSS block.

themes.title: Boundary — Cricket Club HTML Template (green & gold)
themes.oneliner: A complete cricket club website — fixtures with proper scorelines, league table, squad averages, junior section and a first-ball countdown, in classic cricket green & gold.
themes.description:
Your club's website shouldn't look like a football template with the badge swapped. Boundary is built for how cricket clubs actually run — the demo club (Longmead CC, est. 1894, home at The Meadow) reads like a real village club, not lorem ipsum.

- **Match centre**: fixtures and results with real cricket scorelines (187/6 dec, 40-over formats), W/L/D/T badges, and a league table with your club's row highlighted — all plain HTML, edited by copy-paste
- **First-ball countdown** on the homepage (set one date attribute)
- **Squad grid** with batting and bowling roles, player pages with averages, and a "sponsor this player" revenue CTA
- Junior cricket page: All Stars and Dynamos age groups, coaching nights, parents' FAQ
- Match report layout, club history and honours, contact with ground directions
- 8 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA patterns, tested at iPhone widths
- Every colour is a CSS variable — matching your exact club colours is a five-minute job, documented in the README

Perfect for: village and league cricket clubs, junior sections, club volunteers who got handed "the website job".

themes.title: Callout — Skip Hire Website Template (local trades)
themes.oneliner: A complete website for a skip hire firm — built around what actually wins waste work: clear skip sizes and prices, same-day delivery messaging, permit handling, and a phone number everywhere.
themes.description:
Five pages (Home, Skips & Prices, Recent Jobs, About, Get a Skip) with a fully written demo company (Loadstar Skips, Doncaster) so you see a real waste business, not lorem ipsum: skip size cards from 4-yard midis to roll-on-roll-off, licensed-waste-carrier trust strip, recent jobs with sizes and prices, review cards, an areas-covered strip, and a booking form with skip-size and waste-type dropdowns plus prohibited-items notes.

- HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS — open index.html and it just works, no build step
- Rebrand in minutes: every colour and font is a CSS variable
- Vanilla JS only, WCAG-AA accessibility patterns, tested at iPhone widths
- Plain-English README covering setup, rebranding and going live

themes.title: Callout — Scaffolding Website Template (local trades)
themes.oneliner: A complete website for a scaffolding company — CISRS-carded credibility, TG20:21 compliance messaging, recent contracts and a free site-survey form, styled in steel and amber.
themes.description:
Five pages (Home, Services, Recent Contracts, About, Site Survey) with a fully written demo company (Vertex Scaffolding, Birmingham) so you see a real access firm, not lorem ipsum: residential and commercial scaffolding services, temporary roofs, edge protection and inspections, an accreditation trust strip (CISRS, CHAS, £10m public liability), contract case cards with durations, review cards from site managers, and a survey-request form.

- HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS — open index.html and it just works, no build step
- Rebrand in minutes: every colour and font is a CSS variable
- Vanilla JS only, WCAG-AA accessibility patterns, tested at iPhone widths
- Plain-English README covering setup, rebranding and going live

themes.title: Solace — Funeral Directors HTML Template
themes.oneliner: A dignified website for a family funeral directors — 24-hour contact first, plain guidance for families, transparent pricing, and a tone that never sells.
themes.description:
Most templates shout. Solace is quiet on purpose — designed for a family firm (the demo is Fairbourne & Sons, Harrogate, serving families since 1932) where the website's job is to reassure at the hardest moment.

- 24-hour phone line prominent on every page, with "what to do first" guidance written in plain, kind English
- Services explained honestly: traditional funerals, cremation, direct cremation, woodland burial, pre-paid plans, repatriation
- Transparent-pricing section aligned with the CMA's standardised price list requirement for UK funeral directors
- Testimonials page with believable, respectful words from families
- About page: four generations of history, the team, the chapel of rest
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested
- A muted dusk-and-brass palette, changeable via CSS variables in minutes

Perfect for: independent funeral directors, family firms, memorial services.

themes.title: Commons — Village Hall & Community Centre Template
themes.oneliner: A warm website for a village hall — hire rates and booking, a what's-on grid of weekly clubs, facilities and access details, run-by-volunteers friendly.
themes.description:
Thousands of village halls run on a volunteer with a spare evening — Commons is built for exactly that (the demo is Elmsworth Village Hall, a charity-run hall built 1928).

- **Hire the Hall** page: rooms with capacities and an hourly-rates table, resident and regular-club discounts, how booking and deposits work
- **What's On** grid: weekly clubs from pilates to short mat bowls, plus one-off events
- Facilities up front: capacity, kitchen, stage, parking, wheelchair access, hearing loop
- About page: the hall's history, the trustees, the charity number, how to volunteer
- Booking enquiry form with event type, date, room and numbers
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested
- Every colour is a CSS variable — five-minute rebrand, documented in the README

Perfect for: village halls, community centres, church halls, scout huts, parish councils.

themes.title: Swim — Fishing Lakes & Day-Ticket Fishery Template
themes.oneliner: A complete website for a day-ticket fishery — lakes and prices up front, catch reports, venue rules and records, in deep-water blue and reed green.
themes.description:
Anglers check three things before they travel: prices, stock and rules. Swim puts all three on the surface — the demo venue (Willow Pool Fisheries, Shropshire, three lakes on a family farm since 1987) reads like somewhere you'd actually fish.

- **Lakes & Prices**: each lake with its stock, pegs and character (runs water, silvers lake, specimen syndicate), plus a day/evening/season ticket price table
- **Catch Reports** page: recent catches with weights, baits and pegs, and a venue records list
- Day-ticket prices and opening times on the homepage, above the fold
- Rules summary written like a fishery actually writes them (barbless only, unhooking mats, no braid)
- About page: the farm's story, stocking policy, otter fencing, match bookings
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested
- Every colour is a CSS variable — five-minute rebrand, documented in the README

Perfect for: day-ticket fisheries, carp lakes, angling clubs, farm diversification ventures.
